name: Mobile Phase 2 — Command Bus Rewire
outcome_goal: Every path to AUTO_EXECUTE now passes through the capability resolver. _SAFE_AUTO_EXECUTE_TYPES is renamed to _UI_RISK_HINT_TYPES and only UI consumers read it. self_healing.py and data_dictionary_governance_remediation.py route recovery/remediation intents through resolve_capability_grant. gate_evaluations rows record grant_ref + plan_envelope_hash. Approval lifecycle + HTTP endpoints + SSE stream are live.
anti_requirements: Do NOT delete _SAFE_AUTO_EXECUTE_TYPES blindly — rename only.
Do NOT replace the gate_evaluations table; extend it with two new columns only.
Do NOT introduce Redis for SSE pub/sub — in-process fan-out is fine for single-user.
Do NOT widen writes outside the authority and handler surfaces.
Do NOT open /approvals/* routes without the mobile session middleware from Phase 1.
notes: Lifted verbatim from config/cascade/specs/W_mobile_access_plan_20260420.queue.json waves 3-4. Depends on Phase 1 authority spine.

jobs.label: wave3_command_bus_rewire
jobs.description: Rewire control_commands.classify_control_intent to use the capability resolver as the authority; demote _SAFE_AUTO_EXECUTE_TYPES to a UI hint.
jobs.outcome_goal: The only path to AUTO_EXECUTE is through resolve_capability_grant returning covered=True. _SAFE_AUTO_EXECUTE_TYPES is renamed to _UI_RISK_HINT_TYPES and never consulted by classify_control_intent. All callers pass plan_hash.
jobs.prompt:
Rewire the authority path at Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/control_commands.py.

1. Rename _SAFE_AUTO_EXECUTE_TYPES -> _UI_RISK_HINT_TYPES at line 455. Add docstring: 'UI hint only. NOT an authority. classify_control_intent MUST NOT route on this set. Used by approval inbox rendering to color/sort pending items.'

2. Rewrite classify_control_intent (line 822) signature to:
   def classify_control_intent(conn, intent, *, plan_hash, requested_by_ref) -> ControlPolicyDecision.
   Body:
   - If intent.plan_hash is None: raise ValueError('plan_hash required; call plan_envelope.stamp_plan_envelope before classify_control_intent').
   - decision = resolve_capability_grant(conn, intent, plan_hash, requested_by_ref).
   - If decision.covered: return ControlPolicyDecision(mode=AUTO_EXECUTE, risk_level=intent.risk_level, grant_ref=decision.grant_id, reason_code='grant_covers_intent').
   - Else: return ControlPolicyDecision(mode=GATE, risk_level=intent.risk_level, grant_ref=None, reason_code=decision.reason_code). Caller opens approval_request.

3. Add get_ui_risk_hint(intent) -> Literal['auto','gate','threshold'] helper reading _UI_RISK_HINT_TYPES for PWA rendering.

4. Fix every caller of classify_control_intent to pass plan_hash + requested_by_ref. Grep 'classify_control_intent' across Code&DBs/Workflow/ and update each site.

5. Extend ControlPolicyDecision with grant_ref and reason_code. Update to_dict.

6. Update tests at tests/unit/test_control_commands.py: assertions expecting AUTO_EXECUTE must first create a capability_grants row. Add _issue_test_grant helper under tests/unit/_capability_fixtures.py.

Verify _SAFE_AUTO_EXECUTE_TYPES does not appear in the runtime after the rewrite.

jobs.label: wave3_self_healing_rewire
jobs.description: Route FIX_AND_RETRY / RETRY_ESCALATED / SKIP recommendations through the capability resolver.
jobs.outcome_goal: self_healing.py cannot issue an autonomous repair action unless an active grant covers it. RecoveryAction gains a reason_code field when blocked by grant coverage.
jobs.prompt:
Modify Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/self_healing.py around lines 125-130.

1. Add gate_recovery_action(conn, recovery_action, run_id, plan_hash) that:
   - Builds a synthetic ControlIntent representing the recovery (command_type='workflow.retry' for FIX_AND_RETRY/RETRY_ESCALATED, 'workflow.skip_job' for SKIP, 'workflow.cancel' for HALT).
   - Calls resolve_capability_grant on it.
   - If not covered: downgrade RecoveryAction to 'pending_approval' and insert approval_requests via runtime.capability.approval_lifecycle.open_approval_request.

2. Every site returning a RecoveryAction goes through gate_recovery_action first.

3. Remove any autorun_ok implicit-permission pattern.

4. Tests at tests/integration/test_self_healing_capability.py: active grant covering workflow.retry -> FIX_AND_RETRY returned as-is; no grant -> FIX_AND_RETRY becomes pending_approval + approval_requests row appears.

jobs.label: wave3_remediation_rewire
jobs.description: Kill autorun_ok in data_dictionary_governance_remediation; route every remediation command through the resolver.
jobs.outcome_goal: grep autorun_ok Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/data_dictionary_governance_remediation.py returns zero. Every GovernanceRemediationCommand carries a grant_ref field populated by the resolver.
jobs.prompt:
Rewire runtime/data_dictionary_governance_remediation.py.

1. Remove autorun_ok from GovernanceRemediationCommand. Replace with grant_ref: str | None (None = requires approval).
2. Inside the builder path that currently sets autorun_ok=True at line 349, instead call resolve_capability_grant for (command_type, plan_hash). If covered, set grant_ref to the returned grant_id. If not, set grant_ref=None + flag as requiring approval.
3. Downstream consumers (ripgrep) now check grant_ref is not None before executing; if None, open approval_requests row.
4. Update tests at tests/unit/test_data_dictionary_governance_remediation.py — remove autorun_ok assertions, add grant_ref assertions against fixture grants.

jobs.label: wave3_gate_evaluations_extension
jobs.description: Every gate_evaluations row records grant_ref + plan_envelope_hash; existing submission_policy.py evaluate_gate path is extended, not replaced.
jobs.outcome_goal: runtime/workflow/submission_policy.py passes grant_ref + plan_envelope_hash into engine.evaluate_gate, and _insert_gate_evaluation writes both columns. All new rows populated; legacy rows stay NULL.
jobs.prompt:
Extend the existing gate evaluation path.

1. In policy/domain.py (or wherever engine.evaluate_gate lives), add grant_ref and plan_envelope_hash as optional kwargs on GateEvaluation and evaluate_gate.
2. In runtime/workflow/submission_policy.py at line 137-154, read the active grant via resolve_capability_grant and pass grant_ref + plan_envelope_hash into engine.evaluate_gate.
3. Extend _insert_gate_evaluation (grep) to write both columns. Legacy INSERT sites without a grant pass NULL.
4. Tests at tests/integration/test_gate_and_promotion_policy.py: new row has grant_ref when grant is active; block decision also records grant_ref for traceability.

jobs.label: wave4_approval_lifecycle
jobs.description: Build the approval_requests lifecycle: open -> pending -> ratified (issues grant) or revoked.
jobs.outcome_goal: runtime.capability.approval_lifecycle exposes open_approval_request, ratify_approval_request (issues capability_grants on success), revoke_approval_request, expire_stale_requests. Every transition is atomic.
jobs.prompt:
Add Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/capability/approval_lifecycle.py.

- open_approval_request(conn, run_id, job_label, intent, plan_envelope) -> ApprovalRequest: inserts pending row.
- ratify_approval_request(conn, request_id, ratified_by_enrollment_id, step_up_challenge_id) -> CapabilityGrant: atomically (a) verifies step_up_challenge_id created <30s ago and consumed here, (b) UPDATE approval_requests SET status='ratified', decided_at=now(), decided_by=..., resulting_grant_id=NEW_ID WHERE status='pending', (c) INSERT capability_grants with the plan envelope's allowed_operations/write_scope/read_scope/budget_ceiling, expires_at = now() + grant_duration (from intent or default 4h), device_enrollment_id=ratifier.
- revoke_approval_request(conn, request_id, revoked_by) -> ApprovalRequest: transition to 'revoked'; if resulting_grant_id exists, also revoke that grant.
- revoke_grant(conn, grant_id, revoked_by) -> CapabilityGrant: UPDATE SET revoked_at=now().
- expire_stale_requests(conn, ttl_minutes=60) -> int: batch UPDATE old pending rows.

Tests at tests/integration/test_approval_lifecycle.py using rollback-isolated conn for each case.

jobs.label: wave4_approval_endpoints
jobs.description: Expose the approval lifecycle over HTTP for the PWA.
jobs.outcome_goal: curl against POST /approvals/request, GET /approvals/pending, POST /approvals/{id}/ratify, POST /approvals/{id}/revoke works given a valid mobile session cookie.
jobs.prompt:
Add Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/api/handlers/approvals.py with aiohttp handlers. All routes require the mobile session middleware from Phase 1; ratify additionally requires step-up assertion.

- POST /approvals/request — called by runtime (internal), inserts pending row, responds with request_id + SSE notification.
- GET /approvals/pending — returns all pending rows newest-first with plan envelope summary.
- POST /approvals/{request_id}/ratify — body { step_up_challenge_id, step_up_assertion }; verifies step-up, calls ratify_approval_request.
- POST /approvals/{request_id}/revoke — calls revoke_approval_request.
- POST /capability/grants/{grant_id}/revoke — calls revoke_grant.

Register in rest.py. Integration tests at tests/integration/test_approval_endpoints.py for each route.

jobs.label: wave4_sse_approvals_stream
jobs.description: Live-push approval changes to the PWA via SSE.
jobs.outcome_goal: GET /approvals/subscribe returns SSE with 'pending_opened', 'ratified', 'revoked', 'expired' event types.
jobs.prompt:
Add SSE endpoint /approvals/subscribe in surfaces/api/handlers/approvals.py.

- Per-process subscriber set; emit from open_approval_request / ratify / revoke hooks via a local pub-sub adapter (no Redis).
- Heartbeat every 15s.
- Clean up on client disconnect.
- Integration test at tests/integration/test_approvals_sse.py: open subscriber, trigger open_approval_request, receive 'pending_opened' within 1s.

In-process pub-sub is fine; single-user, single-origin.
